title: [Story] Sonar transducer integration testing — frequency stability and mounting validation
text:
Story KES-156. Reporter: Cass Doyle (Payload Systems Engineer). Assignee: Cass Doyle. Status: Done. Priority: High. Created: 2024-07-10. Resolved: 2024-12-01.

Description: Integration testing for the sonar transducer (P-1148) covering frequency stability, depth performance, and mounting validation against DOC-462 (Sonar System ICD).

Key findings during this story:
1. DepthWave transducer frequency drift: 200kHz nominal shifted to 184kHz over 2-hour thermal soak at 35°C — documented in KES-158 (frequency drift bug). This triggered ECR-218 (vendor change to AquaSound Corp).
2. Pool test — AquaSound unit (November 2024): Testing at 0.5m, 1.0m, and 2.0m depth. At 0° cant angle (initial mounting), the transducer near-field null zone at ~0.45m depth coincided with the minimum operational depth of 0.5m, producing non-functional returns at minimum depth. The 7° downward cant of DRW-517 and P-1149 mounting bracket was validated as the correct fix (null zone shifts to 1.2m). Cass Doyle review REV-641 documents this rationale.
3. Frequency stability of AquaSound unit: ±1.2kHz over thermal soak — within spec.

Acceptance: DOC-468 (Sonar Performance Test Report) issued, ECN-315 approved.
